title: [HW][NFC] Add BitWidthTypeInterface for extensible bit width calculations
body:
Extracts `hw::getBitWidth(Type)` functionality into a `BitWidthTypeInterface`, enabling non-HW types (e.g., ESI's Window type) to participate in bit width calculations for operations like `hw.bitcast`.

### Changes

- **New interface** (`HWTypeInterfaces.td`): `BitWidthTypeInterface` with `getBitWidth() -> std::optional<int64_t>`
- **Interface implementations** (`HWTypesImpl.td`, `HWTypes.cpp`): Added to `ArrayType`, `UnpackedArrayType`, `StructType`, `UnionType`, `EnumType`, `TypeAliasType`
- **Updated `getBitWidth(Type)`**: Checks interface first, falls back to `IntegerType` handling

### Example

Types from other dialects can now implement the interface:

```cpp
// In another dialect's type definition (.td)
def MyCustomType : TypeDef<MyDialect, "Custom", [
  DeclareTypeInterfaceMethods<BitWidthTypeInterface>
]> { ... }

// Implementation (.cpp)
std::optional<int64_t> MyCustomType::getBitWidth() const {
  return 32; // or std::nullopt if not statically computable
}
```

API compatibility preserved—no changes required to existing `getBitWidth()` callers.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[HW] Make hw::getBitwidth use an TypeInterface?</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Should we make a `StaticallyComputableWidth` Type interface and get `hw::getBitWidth` to use it? Or potentially replace it?
> 
> The use case here is one might have a `hw.struct` with a field which is not a `hw` type. (As I do with ESI's Window type.) At a minimum, `hw.bitcast` compares the two bitwidths. While said field would not be able to be lowered directly to SystemVerilog though it may get lowered of removed by a later pass.
> 
> IIRC we've always said that the HW dialect should be as open as possible, this is something which isn't terribly open. I seem to recall having this discussion in the past...
> 
> What do people think?</issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>Extract the functionality in `circt::hw::getBitwidth(Type)` into a TypeInterface (a new on specified in ODS), implement it for all of the types currently implemented in getBitwidth, and have the existing getBitwidth use it (to maintain API compatibility).</agent_instructions>
>

- Fixes llvm/circt#9269
